scope.summary: Fix duplicate outgoing messages. Zalo redelivers an account's own sent message through the realtime listener with a DIFFERENT msg_id than the send-response id, so dedupe-by-msg_id stored/rendered the same message twice (once optimistically on send, once on the listener echo). Reconcile the echo by content signature in both layers: the frontend session store merges an incoming outgoing echo into the matching just-sent optimistic message (adopting the authoritative id) instead of appending a second row; the SQLite store skips inserting an outgoing message whose (account_id, thread_id, body) already exists within a short window under another msg_id.
goals: store/: save_message skips an outgoing message when a recent (<=15s) outgoing row with the same thread + body exists under a different msg_id (no duplicate row, no double thread/unread bump)
Frontend session store: appendToBucket reconciles an outgoing echo into the matching recent optimistic message (same thread + body within 15s), upgrading its id rather than adding a row
A genuinely distinct outgoing message in the same thread still persists/renders normally
Offline test proves the store-side echo dedupe across differing msg_ids
nonGoals: Changing the self_listen / listener delivery behavior in the zalo layer
Reconciling non-text message echoes (only text bodies are compared this slice)
Backfilling/removing duplicate rows already written before the fix (a one-off cleanup, not code)

acceptance.description: store/ save_message dedupes an outgoing echo across differing msg_ids; the new offline test save_message_dedupes_outgoing_echo_across_msg_ids passes and distinct outgoing messages still persist.
Frontend builds + type-checks with the appendToBucket echo reconciliation.

Live: sending a message no longer renders/persists twice. Verified by the project owner on the running app after the fix; the SQLite store holds a single outgoing row per sent message (no body stored twice under two msg_ids in a thread).
